backend/main.mo: Empty Motoko actor serving as a placeholder backend with no logic implemented.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report mapping implemented features (theme, hero, about, etc.) to their source files, components, test IDs, and confidence levels.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document containing one-sentence summaries of each frontend source file, describing their purpose and key contents.
frontend/index.css: Base Tailwind CSS entry point defining OKLCH color tokens for light and dark themes including sidebar, chart, and UI variables.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point for the LAW HQ Esports site, mounting the React app into a root div and loading the main TypeScript module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component that assembles all page sections (Navigation, Hero, AboutUs, LeadershipTeam, MissionAndOffers, JoinClan, Discord, Footer) with SectionDividers between them on a deep black background.
frontend/src/components/AboutUs.tsx: About Us section with scroll-reveal animations, red-accented heading, clan description copy, and three stat cards (350+ Members, 2 Leaders, ∞ Ambition) alongside a decorative values panel.
frontend/src/components/Discord.tsx: Discord community section featuring a main invite card with member count and join button, a roles breakdown panel (Members, Competitive Team, Staff), and placeholder social media links.
frontend/src/components/Footer.tsx: Renders the site footer with the LAW HQ brand, smooth-scroll navigation links, social media icons, and a copyright line with a heart icon.
frontend/src/components/Hero.tsx: Full-screen hero section with a background image, animated fade-in title/tagline for LAW HQ, and CTA buttons that smooth-scroll to the Join and About sections.
frontend/src/components/JoinClan.tsx: Recruitment call-to-action section with scroll-reveal animations, requirement chips, and a button linking to the clan's Discord or application flow.
frontend/src/components/LeadershipTeam.t: Displays scroll-animated cards for the two LAW HQ leaders (Founder and Presidential Board) with their tags, roles, and descriptions.
frontend/src/components/MissionAndOffers: Section presenting the clan's mission statement and a grid of five offering cards (tournaments, teammates, leadership, growth, community) with scroll-reveal animations.
frontend/src/components/Navigation.tsx: Fixed top navigation bar with smooth-scroll links, scroll-based background change, and a responsive mobile hamburger menu for the LAW HQ site.
frontend/src/components/SectionDivider.t: Decorative section divider component that renders a clipped dark background shape (diagonal, chevron, or slash) with an optional red accent line.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: React hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor (anonymous or authenticated) using React Query, invalidating dependent queries when the identity changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an InternetIdentityProvider and useInternetIdentity hook for managing DFINITY Internet Identity authentication state, including login, logout, and identity persistence.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Placeholder module with no exports, indicating no backend queries are needed for this static content site.
frontend/src/hooks/useScrollReveal.ts: Custom hook that uses IntersectionObserver to track when a referenced element enters the viewport, returning a visibility flag for scroll-reveal animations.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet importing Google Fonts and Tailwind layers, defining CSS custom properties for the LAW HQ dark/red color theme, typography, glow effects, and scroll-reveal animation utilities.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that mounts the React app wrapped with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration extending the default theme with LAW HQ custom color palettes, fonts, shadows, border radii, and animation keyframes using oklch color values.
project_state.json: Tracks the project state (version 3) for the LAW HQ esports website, documenting the site summary, architectural notes, feature list with IDs, image requirements, and known issues.
